entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Europe warily eyes Russian gas; Zelensky vows retaliation for apartment strike
  • Macron’s Uber dealings may prompt parliamentary inquiry in France
  • World population to reach 8 billion by November even as growth slows
  • Uber wooed Russia’s rich and powerful but failed there anyway
  • As Uber steamrolled into France, Emmanuel Macron was a ‘true ally’
  • Europe braces for gas ‘nightmare’ as pipeline from Russia shuts off
  • Wildfires rage in Portugal as punishing heat wave sweeps Europe
  • The scene after a Russian missile strike on residences in the eastern Ukrainian city of Chasiv Yar
  • Sri Lankan president confirms move to quit as protesters occupy his home
  • Protesting bank depositors in China beaten by mob as police stand by
  • Shinzo Abe’s family holds private wake for slain former Japanese leader
  • Last year, Cubans took to the streets. Now they’re fleeing the island.
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • Shanghai fears a second lockdown as China battles BA.5 outbreaks
  • Strike on apartment complex in eastern Ukraine kills 15, traps more under rubble
  • Four ways to see Shinzo Abe’s legacy
  • Abe’s ruling coalition secures big election win in Japan after his killing
  • Russia’s alternative to McDonald’s facing french fry shortage
  • Nigeria jailbreak raises more questions over insecurity
  • US seeks extradition of Palestinian attacker in Jordan
  • Putin expands fast-track Russian citizenship to all Ukraine
  • Taking selfies, Sri Lankans converge on presidential palace
  • South Africa shocked by bar shootings, police hunt suspects
  • Brazil: leaders call for calm after party official's killing
  • His McDonald’s closed for months due to renovations. He kept paying the staff.
  • Kyrgios wore a red cap to Wimbledon. Here’s why that’s controversial.
  • Buttigieg says officials like Kavanaugh ‘should expect’ public protest
  • Post Politics Now: ‘It matters, but it’s not enough,’ Biden says of new gun law
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Bannon, dangling possible testimony, brings new focus to Jan. 6 role
  • Jan. 6 hearing to focus on Trump’s ‘siren call’ to violent extremists
  • Takeaways from the Uber Files investigation
  • About the Uber Files investigation
  • Mark MacGann, former top executive, comes forward as Uber Files leaker
  • Twitter shares slide after Elon Musk moves to cancel deal
  • Uber promised South Africans better lives but knew drivers risked debt and danger
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • As Musk moves to abandon deal, Twitter faces ‘worst case scenario’
  • You can now preview iOS 16′s editable iMessages and more
  • Hideo Kojima studio threatens legal action over false link to Shinzo Abe killing
  • Is Amazon Prime worth it for you?
  • What’s behind rising rents and what can be done?
  • Homeowner recommends whole house water shut-off valves
  • 3 bedroom townhouse-style condo in Germantown lists below $340,000
  • Frontier Tries to Call Spirit Shareholders’ Bluff
  • Big Tech’s Reckoning Won’t Stop With Uber
  • Elon Musk Isn’t the Only One to Blame for the Twitter Mess
  • Fallen Billionaire Offers a Lens on Xi’s China
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why Delaware Is Where Musk and Twitter Will Face Off in Court
  • How Musk’s Twitter Deal Foundered Over ‘Spam Bots’
  • How Cuba’s investment in writers and artists came back to haunt its regime
  • Mass shootings raise the question: What is a dead victim worth?
  • I asked about abortion at a Texas gun show. The answer I got was grim.
  • As a gay kid, I felt the instinct to hide. I feel it again now.
  • Shorten the war. Send 60 HIMARS to Ukraine.
  • As Latino voters shift right, here’s where Democrats will be hit hardest
  • Our picks for Maryland’s 2022 primary elections
  • Maryland’s gubernatorial primaries look close. But the choices are clear.
  • We should worry what happens if Trump never faces justice, not if he does
  • Donald Trump isn’t worried about Paul Ryan’s crocodile tears
  • Democrats are overreacting about the Supreme Court
  • Cartoon by Mike Smith
  • Biden needs to address the coming crisis between Israel and Palestine
  • Middle East policy is too important to be guided by short-term concerns
  • Confirm Hanes to the Eastern District
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden’s Approval Hits 33 Percent; Democrats Want 2024 Options, Poll Shows
  • Dueling Weaknesses
  • States With Abortion Bans Risk Losing Their Economic Edge
  • Democrats Schedule Votes on Abortion Access, but Lack a Legislative Path
  • F.D.A. to Weigh Over-the-Counter Sale of Birth Control Pills
  • The Right to Travel in a Post-Roe World
  • Hutchinson Testimony Jolts Justice Dept. to Discuss Trump’s Conduct More Openly
  • Cassidy Hutchinson: Why the Jan. 6 Committee Rushed Her Testimony
  • In a Time of Conflict, Ukraine Entrepreneurs Make War Their Business
  • Russia Repeatedly Strikes Ukraine’s Civilians. There’s Always an Excuse.
  • Could Twitter Force Musk to Close the Deal? Here’s What We Know
  • What’s Next in the Elon Musk-Twitter Saga? A Court Battle
  • China’s Economy Stumbles in the Fog of Covid War
  • Where Mantras Trump Medicine, Vaccines Are a ‘Violation’
  • Merrick Garland Should Investigate Trump’s 2020 Election Schemes as a ‘Hub and Spoke’ Conspiracy
  • Male Birth Control Is Needed Post-Roe v. Wade
  • ‘Stay and Fight’ Isn’t So Easy When a Red State Puts Your Child at Risk
  • Boris Johnson and the Complicit Conservatives
  • Even if Republicans Outlaw Abortion, Americans Will Soon Rebel
  • And the Next President Will Be …
  • What a Reckoning at the Supreme Court Could Look Like
  • On Abortion Laws, It All Goes Back to 2010
  • Webb Space Telescope: How to Watch Biden and NASA’s Image Reveal
  • Security Forces in China Attack Protesters Seeking Frozen Funds
  • Texans Warned to Cut Electricity Use Amid Fear of Rolling Blackouts
  • Ukraine Live Updates: No Respite for Civilians Despite Pause in Russia’s Advance
  • Ukrainian Medic’s Months in Russian Cell: Cold, Dirty and Used as a Prop
  • ‘There’s Nothing’: Scarcity Deepens Desperation in Sri Lanka
  • Suspect in Abe Shooting Held a ‘Grudge.’ Scrutiny Falls on a Church.
  • A Year After Mass Protest, Cubans Face Stark Choice: ‘Prison or Exile’
  • Three Cities in China Impose Partial Lockdowns and Macau Shuts Casinos
  • Embattled Leaders in Hiding, Sri Lankans Ask: What’s Next?
  • Cambridge Opens an Inquiry After Student Deaths
  • Putin Extends Fast-Track Russian Citizenship Process to All Ukrainians
  • E.U. Health Agency Recommends Second Covid Booster for Older People
  • A Glimpse Inside a Florentine Silk-Weaving Workshop
  • Your Monday Briefing
  • Brittney Griner Is Honored by Fellow Players at W.N.B.A. All-Star Game
  • War in Ukraine Could Have You Paying More For Hummus
  • Your Monday Briefing: Sri Lanka in Turmoil
  • In Ivory Coast, This Rainforest Is Both Refuge and Junkyard
  • What We Know About the Protests in Sri Lanka
  • In Eastern Ukraine, Attacks Intensify as Russia Readies New Offensive
  • What We Know About the Shinzo Abe Assassination
  • Ukraine Fires Its Ambassador to Germany After Controversial Remarks
  • For Uvalde Families, a Call for Answers Grows Louder
  • After Another Mass Shooting, Questions Loom About the Role of Parents
  • Albuquerque Police Investigate Teenager’s Death in Standoff
  • California Expects Surge in Out-of-State Abortion Patients
  • Washburn Fire Threatens Sequoias in Yosemite National Park
  • Defense Firm Said U.S. Spies Backed Its Bid for Pegasus Spyware Maker
  • Bannon Agrees to Testify to Jan. 6 Panel, in Remarkable About-Face
  • Georgia Republicans Say They’re Sticking With Herschel Walker
  • Germany on Edge as Nord Stream 1 Pipeline Goes Offline for Repair
  • Some Surprising Good News: Bookstores Are Booming and Becoming More Diverse
  • Francis X. Clines, Lyrical Writer for The Times, Dies at 84
  • Twitter’s Stock Falls Further as Doubts Swirl Over Musk’s Takeover
  • Oil Companies Oppose Windfall Tax in Britain Despie Record Profits
  • Does Public Art Have an Afterlife?
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Guardian
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v Norway: Women’s Euro 2022 – live!
  • Biden calls again for US assault rifles ban: ‘We are living in a country awash in weapons of war’ – live
  • England beat South Africa by five wickets in first women’s cricket ODI – live reaction!
  • Tory leadership: candidates will need 20 MP backers to enter contest to succeed Boris Johnson – live
  • University of Cambridge launches inquiry after five suspected suicides
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Zelenskiy plans million-strong army to retake south; death toll rises after Russian strike on apartment block – live
  • Firefighters attempt to save giant sequoias as Yosemite wildfire grows
  • Biden heckled by Parkland father during event to celebrate new gun law
  • Austria’s Naschenweng strikes late to leave Northern Ireland playing for pride
  • Pacific summit: Suva near to bursting as PIF meeting turns Fiji’s pandemic bust to boom
  • Twitter must press case against Elon Musk for takeover termination | Nils Pratley
  • Thomas talks up ‘phenomenal’ Pogacar as Tour de France’s Covid fears ease
  • Texas officials agree to release hallway video from Uvalde school shooting
  • Uber’s privileged access to politicians shows the lobby system urgently needs to change | Vicky Cann
  • Australia a ‘trusted global partner’ on climate again, Albanese to tell energy forum
  • Patience is vital tactic in Ukraine’s hopes of turning tide against Russia
  • Jafar Panahi is third film-maker in a week to be arrested in Iran
  • Monty Norman obituary
  • Ex-US secretary of state Condoleezza Rice joins Broncos ownership group
  • Novak Djokovic tumbles in ATP rankings despite Wimbledon triumph
  • Calls for crackdown on gangs in South Africa after spate of gun attacks
  • How laughing Elon Musk got serious about ending Twitter deal
  • Uber offered shares to media barons for political help, leak reveals
  • The Uber whistleblower: I’m exposing a system that sold people a lie
  • Fries off menu until autumn at some of McDonald’s Russian successor outlets
  • Dior v Valentino: row breaks out after Rome show allegedly blocks boutique
  • Transfer round-up: Pogba returns to Juventus, Doucouré checks in at Palace
  • Sunlight may trigger hormone that makes men hungrier, study suggests
  • It all comes back to ‘darling Peter’: setting Britten and Pears’s love letters to music
  • Bond theme composer Monty Norman dies aged 94
  • The Tory leadership videos are like fever dreams – who on earth are they for? | Zoe Williams
  • Claim to Fame: celeb relatives go undercover in desperate reality show
  • Tory chancers are turning the leadership contest into a wild battle of extremists | Polly Toynbee
  • President of crisis-hit Sri Lanka confirms he will quit amid protests
  • Bernie Ecclestone charged with fraud over £400m assets
  • Biden is trying to rebuild America’s middle class. Our lopsided economy needs it | Heather Cox Richardson
  • Russia-Ukraine war update: what we know on day 138 of the invasion
  • Russia and Belarus face ban from Paris Olympics, warns senior IOC member
  • Why is it so hot in the UK and elsewhere in Europe and what are the dangers?
  • Protest in China over frozen bank accounts ends in violence
  • Gang caught running fake Indian cricket league to dupe Russian gamblers
  • Shinzo Abe killing: ‘Moonies’ church confirms suspect’s mother is member
  • Woman who gave birth in Jamaica stranded after baby refused entry to UK
  • Wolff decries ‘brainless’ F1 fans for Austrian GP abuse as Vettel wants bans
  • First pictures of Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan Neighbours reunion
  • Wimbledon 2022 awards: the final word on the best and worst at SW19
  • Humans need to value nature as well as profits to survive, UN report finds
  • Jenin violence rages two months after death of Al Jazeera journalist
  • US tourist injured falling into Mount Vesuvius crater after taking selfie
  • Uber hired oligarch-linked Russian lobbyist despite bribery fears
  • Harry Styles can get away with wearing a skirt. But can I?
  • Sea mines: the deadly danger lurking in Ukraine’s waters
  • Thor: Love and Thunder – the ‘super-gay’ tone, female Thor and Russell Crowe – discuss with spoilers
  • Dani Alves: ‘Everyone says I’m old but I completely disagree’
  • Log jam: Vancouver wants its beloved beach logs back, but officials are wary
  • Florence Pugh hits back at ‘vulgar’ criticism of her body on social media
  • ‘An oasis of hope’ – how football is a cause for optimism in Palestine
  • Old friend getting on your nerves? I ditched mine – and regretted it for years | Sindhu Vee
  • The Uber files tell a simple truth: democracy depends on curbing mercenary tech giants | Rafael Behr
  • Grace Geyoro hat-trick leads France’s 5-1 rout for perfect Euros start against Italy
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Klarna’s valuation crashes to under $7bn in tough funding round
  • FT interview: Brazil’s Lula on the prospects of an extraordinary comeback
  • Is the US starting to resemble an emerging market?
  • Military briefing: is the west running out of ammunition to supply Ukraine?
  • Live news updates: Zahawi pledges to abolish corporation tax rise next year if he becomes prime minister
  • Dollar surges and stocks tumble as traders weigh global growth outlook
  • Morgan Stanley reshuffles investment bank leadership
  • US banks set for lending earnings boost as Fed lifts rates
  • Merger arbitrage funds go hunting as corporate deals come under threat
  • Twitter: Musk tries to hit edit button on deal
  • Killing of Shinzo Abe shines spotlight on politicians’ links with Moonies
  • Joe Biden seeks reset on Saudi Arabia as oil tensions flare
  • Johnson, Trump and how to get rid of a strongman leader
  • Wealth management booms as the rich get richer but markets get choppy
  • Sri Lanka prime minister confirms besieged president Rajapaksa to resign
  • SEC to rescind Trump-era rule for proxy advisers
  • Russia turns off gas pipeline to Germany for repairs
  • Russian court allows Kazakh oil exports via key pipeline to continue
  • India’s top court sentences tycoon Vijay Mallya to four-month jail term
  • Amazon banks on strong Prime Day sales extravaganza
  • First images from James Webb telescope to show most distant galaxies
  • FBI director warns China espionage is greatest threat to US and allies
  • Tory MPs to set out tight timetable for party’s leadership contest
  • Business vents at UK-EU gridlock on post-Brexit trade
  • Bernie Ecclestone charged with fraud after UK tax investigation
  • European health agencies back second Covid booster for over-60s
  • UK trains face further disruption as drivers vote to strike
  • Keir Starmer adjusts to life after Boris Johnson as Tories pick leader
  • Ministers set to back next wave of City of London reforms
  • Global population growth hits lowest rate since 1950
  • Global business faces perfect storm
  • While politics fragments, Europe is falling into line on trade
  • The Supreme Court has weakened legal predictability in America
  • Zahawi promises to abolish planned corporation tax increase
  • China’s Ganfeng Lithium buys lithium mines in Argentina
  • The changing face of gun owners in the US
  • OECD wants key part of global tax deal signed off by mid-2023
  • Uefa aims to double value of US Champions League media rights
  • Emerging markets: inflation, shortages and interest rates cripple economies
  • Johnson puts on hold plans for windfall levy on UK electricity generators
  • The climate crisis is no excuse for backsliding on democracy
  • China tech stocks: M&A goes on the chopping block
  • Did central bank balance sheets really need to get so big?
  • Gulf of Mexico’s oil riches pose climate question for Biden
  • Ex-Trump adviser Bannon willing to testify before January 6 panel
  • Musk stirs up a legal Twitterstorm
  • These apps have eyes on your work emails
  • Ericsson, Qualcomm and Thales to trial 5G from space
  • STMicro and GlobalFoundries to build chip factory in EU tech independence push
  • Shell takes to TikTok as oil groups try to boost credentials during energy crisis
  • Ukraine war offers South Korea’s Hanwha chance at Nato defence market
  • The best outdoor tech for thrill seeking at home and beyond
  • Twitter hires US law firm Wachtell to sue Musk for ending $44bn takeover
  • Frontline and Euronav tweak $4bn merger terms in face of opposition
  • European ESG funds buy up defence and energy stocks
  • Chinese stocks drop on threat of renewed Covid lockdowns
  • Crypto: confirmed casino
  • Financial engineering to be put on trial in bankruptcy courts
  • Pressure grows on regulators to scrutinise ESG data providers
  • Crypto collapse hits black Americans hard
  • Environment books round-up — the generation facing the brunt of climate change
  • European travel/Dufry: investors should pack lightly in this sector
  • Private equity: capital call financing speeds up deals
  • Banks as recession-o-meters
  • How can I move from being a specialist police detective to financial services?
  • ‘The government is looking for the next Lex Greensill’

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 11 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-11 19:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tech giants back Australia’s first solar-powered carbon capture project
  • ‘Just an honour to be part of it’: Arnell becomes first former player to coach in AFLW
  • ‘New Zealand got it wrong’: The man vying to topple Jacinda Ardern and open up the nation
  • The run home: Where the contenders stand in the race for the top eight
  • World population to peak and decline sooner than previously thought
  • ‘Total disbelief’: Unnecessary medical test killed Carmel Haynes
  • ‘We are just going to be drowning’: Elected surgery delays projected to rise
  • Abe’s legacy? Japan a step closer to reawakening warrior spirit
  • Clouds form for a new government with a busy agenda
  • We must defend our ancient history from further desecration at Murujuga
  • No ‘presenteeism’: Small businesses pause office return as winter COVID-19 wave worsens
  • The company repairing old sneakers so you don’t have to throw them away
  • Clarity and calm: Queensland insist they can see through COVID fog
  • Free trade deal with Europe can be served up in 12 months: trade minister
  • Barilaro unlikely to be the only loser as New York job saga rolls on
  • Murdoch’s new bookie shop off and racing
  • More learned in Galle defeat than victory: Cummins
  • Playing piano and swimming, Sri Lankan protesters settle into presidential palace
  • Coaches, ‘nesting’ and amicable splits: WA divorce in the modern age
  • From hills to coast, Perth suburbs see sales growth jump
  • Albanese aims for superpower status in global energy transition
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 12, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 11, 2022
  • Could climate change help thaw relations between Beijing and Canberra?
  • Antivirals under the radar as drug company rolls out own COVID health campaign
  • Skills crisis the ‘number one challenge’ for employers at jobs summit
  • Australia prepares for likelihood of climate refugees, backs push for UN justice
  • Albanese wants to borrow a page from Hawke’s playbook
  • ‘If we can move quickly, we will’: Jobs summit reforms could happen this year
  • Catholic women want to be at the decision table
  • In the Herald: July 12, 1955
  • Cutting through the invisible barriers
  • Private and independent schools awarded vast majority of $30,000 Ramsay Centre scholarships
  • ICAC considers probe into John Barilaro’s US trade post
  • ‘Terrible tragedy’: Inquest examines stabbing death of Sydney teacher
  • House destroyed by landslip on the Hawkesbury River
  • Judge hopes to deliver verdict ‘relatively quickly’ as Dawson trial ends
  • Two dead after three ‘unrelated’ units catch fire across Sydney
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Anxiety grows for harvest trapped in a war zone
  • America decided the pandemic was over. The coronavirus has other ideas
  • ‘Undeniable’ flaws in Abe’s security, Japan police admit as party secures election win
  • Steve Bannon, facing jail, agrees to testify to Jan 6 committee
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Tuesday, July 12
  • Perth doctors make IVF breakthrough
  • Fears for three fisherman still missing off Queensland coast
  • Casino boss concedes some issues heard by NSW probe ‘would apply’ in Qld
  • Stood-down cop’s outburst leads him back to Brisbane court
  • Staff seconded elsewhere despite police battling rising domestic violence cases
  • Police forced to shoot dog after it attacked family
  • Murder charge after man dies at Brisbane's Fortitude Valley train station
  • Police find replica gun, sword and axe after pursuit in Adelaide
  • Travellers stranded as Brisbane Airport sees busiest day since pandemic
  • NAB to defend trader’s unequal pay complaint
  • ‘Quality issues’: Bad oranges bruise Costa Group shares
  • EML Payments shares plunge as CEO Tom Cregan departs
  • ‘Unconscionable’: Insurer preyed on Indigenous customers
  • FDA decision demolishes rapid tester's share price
  • Has the sharemarket selloff of 2022 hit a bottom? Some market bulls think so
  • Glencore ‘proud’ as coal surpasses iron ore as nation’s biggest export
  • The problem with Elon Musk’s high stakes plan to pull out of $US44bn Twitter deal
  • Property services group Link rejects $2.3b Dye & Durham takeover offer
  • The Wrap: Tech and miners drag shaky ASX 1.1 per cent lower
  • ‘Kill switch’: Uber used stealth tech, lobbied world leaders to avoid regulation: report
  • How social investing is changing the way Australians trade
  • The ‘scary’ new role bringing Michael Sheen to Australia
  • Julia Gillard to take ground-breaking misogyny speech to the stage
  • Wedding Singer musical romps home with a swag of Green Room gongs
  • Torn in half by war, Vasily Petrenko sees hope for peace in music
  • Forty years on, Blade Runner’s still peerless as a sci-fi classic
  • First pictures of Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan reunion on Neighbours revealed
  • We’re a disagreeable country and arguing is a national pastime
  • Thor’s thumping opening shows how Marvel is still dominating cinemas
  • The old lady in Titanic is my spiteful, minimalist queen
  • The world gets worse, but public libraries are forever
  • Why Leonard Cohen’s Hallelujah endures 56 years since it was written
  • Want to learn more about Roe v Wade? Here’s the podcast for you
  • Australian podcasters sign exclusive, multimillion-dollar deal with Spotify
  • Trailer for new Aussie Netflix series 'Surviving Summer'
  • ‘This is my story’: Sydney’s controversial rappers defend their music
  • Off-screen and on, Jeff Bridges faces his own mortality
  • Don't Worry Darling (2022) | Official Trailer
  • What makes Dorian Gray such a hit? The music is part of the secret
  • We need to talk about Russell Crowe’s accent in Thor (and everything else)
  • Greens’ pitch to ditch gas signals energy as an election issue
  • National weather forecast for Monday July 11
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-11 18:52PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Let Hong Kong be Hong Kong’, top US diplomat to city tells Beijing in speech critical of central government
  • Hong Kong considers tougher penalties for residents caught feeding wild pigeons
  • Coronavirus: Care homes in Hong Kong struggle to find staff as they brace for possible sixth wave of infections
  • Macau’s citywide coronavirus lockdown confuses and frustrates residents
  • Hong Kong sweats on hottest day of year so far as temperature hits 37.4 degrees Celsius in some parts of city
  • Hong Kong’s John Lee to meet lawmakers for informal exchange on Wednesday, as some legislators raise concerns over transparency
  • Hong Kong residents pay tribute to ‘great, tough’ Shinzo Abe at Japanese consulate
  • Hong Kong police arrest driver suspected of hitting cat with car and fleeing
  • Hong Kong ranks 7th among places where people are most likely to live beyond 100 years
  • Covid-19: Hong Kong to step up restriction enforcement with colour-coded app system, but health minister says change not aimed at ‘tracking’ residents
  • Despite presenting many challenges, pandemic-era remote learning also helped parents better understand their child’s ability and focus, as well as gain more appreciation for the work of teachers
  • As fuel prices shoot up, Hong Kong taxi drivers work smarter, other motorists choose to leave the car at home
  • As BA.5 fuels another surge in cases, a new Omicron subvariant is also on the march
  • Chinese scientists develop gravity detector with potential use in submarine navigation
  • Shinzo Abe funeral: Taiwanese vice-president in Tokyo but no word from Beijing on its envoy
  • China’s cash crisis culprit named by police, said to have run powerful criminal gang since 2011
  • TikTok owner ByteDance to launch new social media app for young users that will rival Instagram-like service Xiaohongshu
  • China’s foreign minister Wang Yi warns of ‘ferocious storms’ if one-China policy is abandoned by US and Taipei
  • China and Pakistan launch naval drills aimed at countering US Indo-Pacific strategy
  • Chinese smartphone maker Oppo loses to Nokia in patent infringement case in Germany, potentially affecting sales
  • Former Chinese police boss Fu Zhenghua to face court on corruption charges
  • Tencent, Alibaba and Didi fined by China antitrust watchdog for unreported merger deals as early as 2011
  • Cholera infection puts China’s Wuhan University on high alert
  • China’s car, internet and property sectors to rebound in second half on back of regulatory easing, stimulus measures, Credit Suisse says
  • Should Nato be worried about China and Russia’s influence in Africa?
  • White-shirted group attacks protesters at China banking scandal demonstration
  • Chinese workers ditch cramped cubicles for remote locales, freedom – and just look at that view
  • Climate change, nuclear catastrophe, out-of-control AI: how US-China tech rivalry puts humanity at risk
  • Japan’s energy crisis, Covid-19 woes likely to eclipse LDP’s push to amend constitution
  • Controversial church says mother of suspect who shot Shinzo Abe is a member
  • Shinzo Abe to have private funeral, with separate ceremony for friends of Japan that may include Trump
  • China-Australia relations: Albanese rejects Beijing’s 4 ‘demands’ after Wang Yi-Penny Wong meeting
  • Pro-Beijing Kiribati quits Pacific Islands Forum as US-China tensions overshadow summit
  • Viral road rage outburst on Malaysia-Singapore bridge sparks police investigation
  • Chinese-built surveillance systems spread across junta-ruled Myanmar, including hundreds of Huawei cameras: sources
  • US woman’s TikTok on ‘third world’ Malaysia stirs debate about prejudice, misconceptions
  • Antony Blinken makes unexpected stop in Tokyo to pay respects after Shinzo Abe assassination
  • Vladimir Putin expands fast track Russian citizenship to all Ukraine
  • McDonald’s Russian successor hit by sanction shortages amid Ukraine war, meaning ‘no fries with that’
  • Nutty idea? Contraceptives for squirrels in UK being mulled by scientists
  • Trump says ‘life begins at 80’ but not for Biden, amid fears President’s age is affecting his leadership
  • Travel chaos: UK’s busiest airport warns of further cancellations following 40 years of passengers in 4 months
  • Russia will intensify Donbas attack as more bodies found – and Europe feels repercussions
  • Europe frets over reduced Russian gas supply. Will Putin turn off the tap?
  • US weighs possible resumption of offensive arms sales to Saudi Arabia
  • Nasa to reveal James Webb Space Telescope’s first full-colour, ‘deepest images of the Universe’
  • Uber lobbied politicians, broke laws in global push: investigation
  • French opposition denounces reports of ‘secret deal’ between Uber and Emmanuel Macron
  • UK foreign minister Liz Truss joins 11-strong race to replace Boris Johnson
  • Ghana fears Nigeria jail break jihadists may try to enter country
  • Ukraine ‘deeply disappointed’ after Canada exempts Russian gas turbine from sanctions
  • Israeli ministers Gideon Saar and Benny Gantz to run as partners in upcoming elections
  • Russian gas cuts to Europe are pushing Germany towards Asian coal, but the demand won’t last
  • Morningstar finds ‘varying levels of commitment’ on setting net zero emission targets among world’s top fund managers
  • China’s new ESG disclosure guidelines need a forward-looking slant for international alignment, experts say
  • Hong Kong risks losing companies to ‘stickiness’ in other cities, threatening its international hub status, advisor warns
  • Demand for Singapore rental property is surging amid supply shortage and expat arrivals
  • Hong Kong stocks see sharpest drop in a month amid Shanghai, Macau Covid-19 flare-ups, fines on Alibaba, Tencent, Bilibili
  • Swap Connect with Hong Kong will help China attract more international investors to its bond markets, Standard Chartered banker says
  • Green stocks such as BYD and CATL are red hot again, as Chinese stocks flirt with bull market
  • Can Hong Kong become an innovation hub for China if closed borders remain the norm? Industry executives see challenges
  • Why school leadership is a driving force for innovations in education
  • Netflix K-drama Extraordinary Attorney Woo: heartwarming legal drama starring Park Eun-bin really is extraordinary
  • Art Basel, Documenta and Venice Biennale gatecrashed by maverick art activist who critiques exclusivity of the art world
  • Pastel artist Nicolas Party’s vivid landscapes a reflection on our changing relationship with the natural world
  • What is a nap dress? Floral smocked dresses are popular post-Covid as women seek comfort and joy in what they wear
  • Ballad of a White Cow movie review: Maryam Moghadam’s haunting Iranian drama about a widow battling injustice
  • Singapore arts pioneer Tan Boon Hui’s death ‘a tremendous blow’ to Asian art
  • What is ‘face blindness’ and why does Brad Pitt say people hate him because of it?
  • Abortion in ancient China with TCM and acupuncture was unreliable – most women resorted to physically injuring themselves, as in one harrowing story
  • Why most overseas visitors to Japan are on business trips, with the country opening up cautiously
  • Who is Cameron Norrie, Britain’s handsome No 1 tennis star? The leftie who lost to Novak Djokovic was born in South Africa, has an American girlfriend and has earned over US$6.3 million in prize money
  • What do all 3 of Elon Musk’s baby mamas have in common? Meet Shivon Zilis, the Neuralink tech whizz who shares twins with the Tesla CEO, recent girlfriend Grimes and author Justine Wilson
  • 6 female child stars who quit Hollywood – and what they did next: from Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen to The Amanda Show’s Amanda Bynes and iCarly’s Jennette McCurdy
  • Meet Marc Kalman, Bella Hadid’s elusive art director boyfriend – the supermodel’s new partner has been described as ‘a young Fabien Baron, Charles Saatchi and Steve Jobs rolled into one’
  • 7 K-drama stars who just became mums: besides Son Ye-jin’s pregnancy news, Kang So-ra, Choi Ji-woo, Park Shin-hye and Taeyang’s wifey Min Hyo-rin are all recent proud mamas
  • How Olivier Rousteing paid homage to Jean Paul Gaultier: the Balmain designer’s one-off haute couture collection for Paris Fashion Week was tongue-in-cheek and full of drama
  • Meet Sonoya Mizuno, star of the Game of Thrones prequel: she danced alongside Emma Stone in La La Land and modelled for an Adidas campaign, but most will know her from Crazy Rich Asians
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
